categories.content: Use this chapter as a question-driven entry point into the SDV Landscape. Prefer a chat-style prompt? Open the Ask the SDV Landscape assistant for an in-browser Q&A flow, then use the linked sections below to compare adjacent Eclipse SDV projects in more detail.

subcategories.subcategory: I need a development toolchain for vehicle applications
subcategories.content:
Start with Tooling Devops / Development.

Good first matches include Eclipse Velocitas for end-to-end in-vehicle application development, Eclipse Open Vehicle API for building a vehicle abstraction interface, and Eclipse SDV Developer Console when you need lifecycle support from development to release.

subcategories.subcategory: I need simulation, SIL/HIL, or automated validation
subcategories.content:
Start with Tooling Devops / Testing & Validation.

The main matches are Eclipse OpenXilEnv for lightweight SIL/HIL execution on a PC and Eclipse openDuT for automated testing and validation workflows.

subcategories.subcategory: I need in-vehicle data access or service-to-service communication
subcategories.content:
Start with Service Fabric and Communication / Middleware & Frameworks, Communication Protocols, or Service Mesh.

Typical matches are Eclipse Kuksa for the data orchestration layer, Eclipse Chariott and Eclipse eCAL for middleware, Eclipse Zenoh and Eclipse iceoryx for communication, and Eclipse uProtocol or Eclipse OpenSOVD for service-oriented integration.

subcategories.subcategory: I need orchestration, deployment, or software updates in the vehicle
subcategories.content:
Start with Tooling Devops / Deployment and Runtime and Orchestration / Workload & Container Orchestration.

Relevant projects include Eclipse Muto for adaptive deployment, Eclipse Ankaios for workload management across nodes and VMs, Eclipse BlueChi™ for deterministic multi-node service control, and Eclipse Pullpiri or Eclipse Symphony for orchestration scenarios.

subcategories.subcategory: I need a full SDV stack, reference platform, or blueprint
subcategories.content:
Start with Distributions and Frameworks / Distributions and Example Applications.

Good starting points are Eclipse Leda for combining SDV components into a coherent whole, Eclipse Safe Open Vehicle Core for an open core stack, Eclipse Automotive API Framework for reusable building blocks, and Eclipse SDV Blueprints for applied reference examples.

subcategories.subcategory: I want to explore AI capabilities for SDV
subcategories.content:
Start with AI / AI.

The current match is Eclipse LMOS, a Language Model Operating System platform for building and running AI systems in the SDV ecosystem.

categories.content:
The AI category includes technologies that enable artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities in Software-Defined Vehicles.

It supports data-driven vehicle functions such as perception, prediction, and optimization across in-vehicle and cloud environments.

subcategories.content: See main description

categories.content:
Tooling & DevOps covers all tools and platforms that support the complete lifecycle of Software-Defined Vehicles (SDVs) – from architecture and development to testing, deployment, and operations.

This category addresses the increasing complexity of SDV systems and enables scalable, automated, and repeatable development processes across teams and organizations.

subcategories.content:
Design tools support architecture, system, and software design for Software-Defined Vehicles. They act as a “single source of truth” for system models and enable early validation of dependencies, resource usage, safety, and security aspects.

Typical use cases include model-based systems engineering (MBSE), architecture reviews, and system-level simulations.

Development tools accelerate the implementation of in-vehicle software and cloud-based vehicle services. They provide SDKs, frameworks, CLIs, and development environments for modern, service-oriented vehicle software.

The primary focus is on developer experience, reusability, and cloud-native development workflows.

This subcategory includes tools for simulation, test automation, and validation of vehicle software. It supports SIL, HIL, and virtual test environments to enable early detection of defects.

Testing & Validation is a key enabler for continuous integration and functional safety in SDV architectures.

The Process & Frameworks category covers methodologies, reference frameworks, and governance models that support the structured development, operation, and evolution of Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) platforms.

It includes processes for software lifecycle management, collaboration, compliance, and standardization, as well as frameworks that enable consistent implementation across projects and organizations.

Process & Frameworks provide the foundation for scalable development, interoperability, and long-term maintainability of SDV ecosystems.

Deployment solutions enable the secure rollout, update, and management of software on vehicles and edge systems. This includes OTA mechanisms, application deployment, and lifecycle management.

They form the bridge between cloud-based development and in-vehicle runtime environments.

categories.content:
This category describes the foundational communication and integration layer of Software-Defined Vehicles. It ensures that services, ECUs, and external systems can communicate efficiently, securely, and at scale.

Service Fabric and Communication components are essential for building modular, distributed, and service-oriented SDV platforms.

subcategories.content:
Middleware abstracts underlying vehicle hardware and communication mechanisms. It provides unified APIs for data access, service interaction, and vehicle functions.

Frameworks in this subcategory form the basis for portable and service-oriented vehicle software architectures.

The Service Mesh category covers technologies that manage and secure service-to-service communication within Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) platforms.

A service mesh provides dedicated infrastructure capabilities such as service discovery, traffic management, observability, and policy enforcement, independent of application logic.

In SDV architectures, service meshes enable reliable and scalable communication between distributed vehicle services across ECUs, zones, edge nodes, and cloud environments, while supporting security, resilience, and operational visibility.

Communication protocols define how data and services are exchanged between vehicle components, edge nodes, and cloud systems.

The focus is on scalability, real-time capabilities, and transport-agnostic communication.

Digital Twin technologies represent the state and capabilities of a vehicle or vehicle system in digital form. They enable simulation, analysis, and control throughout the entire lifecycle.

Digital twins are a key concept for modern Software-Defined Vehicle platforms and cloud-to-vehicle integration.

categories.content:
This category focuses on the execution of software inside the vehicle. It includes runtime environments, orchestration, and service management across multiple ECUs and computing platforms.

Runtime and Orchestration solutions enable dynamic, flexible, and maintainable SDV software deployments.

subcategories.content:
Orchestration platforms manage the execution, scaling, and interaction of software workloads in the vehicle. They enable containerized and service-oriented architectures even in resource-constrained environments.

The goal is deterministic, secure, and manageable operation of Software-Defined Vehicle software.

categories.content:
This category includes complete software stacks, reference platforms, and frameworks for Software-Defined Vehicles. They combine operating systems, middleware, and management components into integrated SDV platforms.

Distributions and frameworks accelerate adoption and provide a solid foundation for building SDV solutions.

subcategories.content: This category includes complete software stacks, reference platforms, and frameworks for Software-Defined Vehicles. They combine operating systems, middleware, and management components into integrated SDV platforms.

categories.content:
Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS) provide deterministic execution, low latency, and high reliability for safety-critical vehicle functions.

RTOS form the foundation for time-critical control and real-time applications in Software-Defined Vehicles.

subcategories.content: See main description

categories.content:
Example Applications demonstrate concrete use cases and reference implementations for Software-Defined Vehicles. They show how SDV building blocks can be combined in real-world scenarios.

They serve as learning resources and entry points for developers, architects, and system integrators.

subcategories.content: See main description
